sections.paragraph: 1Ki 9:18 קרי, and 2Ch 8:4 [Tadmor], pr.n. of a city built by Solomon, in a fertile district of the Syrian desert, between Damascus and the Euphrates, called by the Arabs, and now bearing the name تَدْمُر or تَتْمُر (i.e. a place abounding in palms, see Schultens, Ind. ad Vit. Salad.), Gr. Palmyra (just as on the other hand the Arabians called Palma, a city in Spain تدمياة). The same is תָּמָד (palm), loc. cit. כתיב, which seems to have been less used. In the Aramean and Greek inscriptions which are found in great numbers in the ruins of Palmyra, the name is spelled either תדמד or תדמור, see Swinton, Philos. Transactions, vol. xlviii.
